author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …“Bless the Lord, O my soul! and all that is within me, His holy Name” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I suppose that he speaketh not of what is within the body; I do not suppose him to mean this, that our lungs and liver, and so forth, are to burst forth into the voice of blessing of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are lungs in our breast indeed, like a kind of bellows, which send forth successive breathings, which breathing forth of the air inhaled is pressed out into voice and sound, when the words are articulated; nor can any utterance sound forth from our mouth, but what the pressed lungs have given vent to; but this is not the meaning here; all this relateth to the ears of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath ears: the heart also hath a voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man speaketh to the things within him, that they may bless God, and saith unto them, “all that is within me bless His holy Name!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou ask the meaning of what is within thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy soul itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In saying then, “all that is within me, bless His holy Name,” it only repeateth the above, “Bless the Lord, O my soul:” for the word “Bless,” is understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cry out with thy voice, if there be a man to hear; hush thy voice, when there is no man to hear thee; there is never wanting one to hear all that is within thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessing therefore hath already been uttered from our mouth, when we were chanting these very words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We sung as much as sufficed for the time, and were then silent: ought our hearts within us to be silent to the blessing of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the sound of our voices bless Him at intervals, alternately, let the voice of our hearts be perpetual.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou comest to church to recite a hymn, thy voice soundeth forth the praises of God: thou hast sung as far as thou couldest, thou hast left the church; let thy soul sound the praises of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art engaged in thy daily work: let thy soul praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art taking food; see what the Apostle saith: “Whether ye eat or drink, do all to the glory of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I venture to say; when thou sleepest, let thy soul praise the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not thoughts of crime arouse thee, let not the contrivances of thieving arouse thee, let not arranged plans of corrupt dealing arouse thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy innocence even when thou art sleeping is the voice of thy soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all His rewards” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the rewards of the Lord cannot be before thine eyes unless thy sins are before thine eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not delight in past sin be before thine eyes, but let the condemnation of sin be before thine eyes: condemnation from thee, forgiveness from God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus God rewardeth thee, so that thou mayest say, “How shall I reward the Lord for all His rewards unto me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This it was that the martyrs considering (whose memory we are this day celebrating), and all the saints who have despised this life, and as ye have heard in the Epistle of St. John, laid down their lives for the brethren, which is the perfection of love, even as our Lord saith: “Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends:” this the holy martyrs, then, considering, despised their lives here, that they might find them there, following our Lord’s words when He said, “He that loveth his life, shall lose it; and he that loseth his life for My sake, shall keep it unto life eternal.”…“Forget not,” he saith, “all His rewards:” not awards, but “rewards.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For something else was due, and what was not due hath been paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence also these words: “What,” he asketh, “shall I reward the Lord for all His rewards unto me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast rewarded good with evil; He rewardeth evil with good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How hast thou, O man, rewarded thy God with evil for good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou who hast once been a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious, hast rewarded blasphemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what good things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First, because thou art: but a stone also is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Next, because thou livest: but a brute also liveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What reward wilt thou give the Lord, for His having created thee above all the cattle; and above all the fowls of the air, in His image and likeness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seek not how to reward Him: give back unto Him His own image: He requireth no more; He demandeth His own coin.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think thou, soul, of all the rewards of God, in thinking over all thy wicked deeds: for as many as are thy sins, so many are His rewards of good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what present, what offering, what sacrifice, canst thou ever tender unto Him?…What wilt thou reward the Lord with?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou wast reflecting, and couldest not find: “I will receive the cup of salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What? hath not the Lord Himself given the cup of salvation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reward Him from thine own, if thou canst.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would say, No, do it not; reward Him not from thine own; God doth not will to be rewarded from thine own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou rewardest Him from thine own, thou rewardest sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all that thou hast thou hast from Him: sins only thou hast of thine own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not wish to be rewarded from thine, He doth will from His own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as, if thou shouldest bring to a husbandman, from the land which he hath sown, an ear of wheat, thou hast rewarded him from the husbandman’s own produce; if thorns, that hast offered him of thine own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reward truth, in truth praise the Lord: if thou shalt choose to reward Him from thine own, thou wilt lie.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who speaketh a lie, speaketh of his own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he who speaketh a lie, speaketh of his own: so he who speaketh truth, speaketh of the Lord’s.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is to receive the cup of salvation, but to imitate the Passion of our Lord?…I will receive the cup of Christ, I will drink of our Lord’s Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beware that thou fail not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, “I will call upon the Name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then who failed, called not upon the Lord; they presumed in their own strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou so return, as remembering that thou art returning what thou hast received.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then let thy soul bless the Lord, as not to forget all His rewards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear ye all His rewards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who forgiveth all thy sin: who healeth all thine infirmities” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold His rewards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, save punishment, was due unto the sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was due to the blasphemer, but the hell of burning fire?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He gave not these rewards: that thou mayest not shudder with dread: and without love fear Him.…But thou art a sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Turn again, and receive these His rewards: He “forgiveth all thy sin.”…Yet even after remission of sins the soul herself is shaken by certain passions; still is she amid the dangers of temptation, still is she pleased with certain suggestions; with some she is not pleased, and sometimes she consenteth unto some of those with which she is pleased: she is taken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is infirmity: but He “healeth all thine infirmities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All thine infirmities shall be healed: fear not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are great, thou wilt say: but the Physician is greater.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No infirmity cometh before the Almighty Physician as incurable: only suffer thou thyself to be healed: repel not His hands; He knoweth how to deal with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not only pleased when He cherisheth thee, but also bear with Him when He useth the knife: bear the pain of the remedy, reflecting on thy future health.…Thou dost not endure in uncertainty: He who promised thee health, cannot be deceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The physician is often deceived: and promiseth health in the human body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is he deceived?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he is not healing his own creature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God made thy body, God made thy soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knoweth how to restore what He hath made, He knoweth how to fashion again what He hath already fashioned: do thou only be patient beneath the Physician’s hands: for He hateth one who rejects His hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This doth not happen with the hands of a human physician.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who redeemeth thy life from corruption” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, “the body which is corrupted, weigheth down the soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The soul then hath life in a corruptible body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It suffereth burdens, it beareth weights.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great obstacles are there to thinking of God Himself, as it is right that men should think of God, as if interrupting us from the necessity of human corruption? how many influences recall us, how many interrupt, how many withdraw the mind when fixed on high? what a crowd of illusions, what tribes of suggestions?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All this in the human heart, as it were, teemeth with the worms of human corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have set forth the greatness of the disease, let us also praise the Physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall not He then heal thee, who made thee such as to be in health, hadst thou chosen to keep the law of health which thou hadst received?…First think of thine own health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes a man is stricken in his own house, on his bed, with a more than usually manifest disorder; although this disorder too, which men dislike to contemplate, be plain; yet each man may be attacked with that sickness for which human physicians are sought, and may gasp with fever in his bed; perhaps he may wish to consider of his domestic affairs, to make some order or disposition relating to his estate or his house; at once he is recalled from such cares by the anxiety of his friends, plainly expressed around him, and he is advised to dismiss these subjects, and first to take thought for his health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then is addressed unto thee, and to all men: if thou art not sick, think of other things: if thy very infirmity prove thee sick, first take heed of thy health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ is thy health: think therefore of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Receive the cup of His saving Health, “who healeth all thine infirmities;” if thou shalt choose, thou shalt gain this Health.…For thy life hath been redeemed from corruption: rest secure now: the contract of good faith hath been entered upon; no man deceives, no man circumvents, no man oppresses, thy Redeemer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath here made a barter, He hath already paid the price, He hath poured forth His blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The only Son of God, I say, hath shed His blood for us: O soul, raise thyself, thou art of so great price.…“He redeemeth thy life from corruption.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who crowneth thee with mercy and loving-kindness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hadst perhaps begun to be in a manner proud, when thou didst hear the words, “He crowneth thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am then great, I have then wrestled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By whose strength?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By thine, but supplied by Him.…He crowneth thee, because He is crowning His own gifts, not thy deservings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I laboured more abundantly than they all,” said the Apostle; but see what he addeth: “yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.” …It is then by His mercy that thou art crowned; in nothing be proud; ever praise the Lord; forget not all His rewards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a reward when thou, a sinner and an ungodly man, hast been called, that thou mayest be justified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a reward, when thou art raised up and guided, that thou mayest not fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a reward, when strength is given thee, that thou mayest persevere unto the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a reward, that even that flesh of thine by which thou wast oppressed riseth again and that not even a hair of thy head perisheth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a reward, that after thy resurrection thou art crowned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a reward, that thou mayest praise God Himself for evermore without ceasing.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After the battle, then, I shall be crowned; after the crown, what shall I do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He who satisfieth thy longing with good things” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5).…Seek thy own good, O soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For one thing is good to one creature, another to another, and all creatures have a certain good of their own, to the completeness and perfection of their nature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a difference as to what is essential to each imperfect thing, in order that it may be made perfect; seek for thy own good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is none good but One, that is, God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The highest good is thy good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is wanting unto him to whom the highest good is good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are inferior goods, which are good to different creatures respectively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, brethren, is good unto the cattle, save to fill the belly, to prevent want, to sleep, to indulge themselves, to exist, to be in health, to propagate?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is good to them: and within certain bounds it hath an allotted measure of good, granted by God, the Creator of all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou seek such a good as this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God giveth also this: but do not pursue it alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Canst thou, a coheir of Christ, rejoice in fellowship with cattle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Raise thy hope to the good of all goods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will be thy good, by whom thou in thy kind hast been made good, and by whom all things in their kind were made good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God made all things very good.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When shall my longing be satisfied with good things? when, dost thou ask?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy youth shall be renewed as the eagle’s.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou then ask when thy soul is to be satisfied with good things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thy youth shall be restored.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he addeth, as an eagle’s.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Something here lieth hidden; what however is said of the eagle, we will not pass over silently, since it is not foreign to our purpose to understand it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let this only be impressed upon our hearts, that it is not said without cause by the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it hath intimated unto us a sort of resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed the youth of the eagle is restored, but not into immortality, for a similitude hath been given, as far as it could be drawn from a thing mortal to signify a thing immortal, not to demonstrate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The eagle is said, after it becometh overpowered with bodily age, to be incapable of taking food from the immoderate length of its beak, which is always increasing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For after the upper part of its beak, which forms a crook above the lower part, hath increased from old age to an immoderate length, the length of this increase will not allow of its opening its mouth, so as to form any interval between the lower beak and the crook above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unless there be such an opening, it hath no power of biting like a forceps, by which to shear off what it may put within its jaws.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The upper part therefore increasing, and being too far hooked over, it cannot open its mouth, and take any food.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This old age doth to it, it is weighed down with the infirmity of age, and becometh too weak from want of power to eat; two causes of infirmity assaulting it, old age, and want.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By a natural device, therefore, in order in some measure to restore its youth, the eagle is said to dash and strike against a rock the upper lip of its beak, by the too great increase of which the opening for eating is closed: and by thus rubbing it against the rock, it breaketh off the weight of its old beak, which impeded its taking food.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It cometh to its food, and everything is restored: it will be after its old age like a young eagle; the vigour of all its limbs returneth, the lustre of its plumage, the guidance of its wings, it flieth aloft as before, a sort of resurrection taketh place in it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is the object of the similitude, like that of the Moon, which after waning and being apparently intercepted, again is renewed, and becometh full; and signifieth to us the resurrection; but when it is full it doth not remain so; again it waneth, that the signification may never cease.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus also what hath here been said of the eagle: the eagle is not restored unto immortality, but we are unto eternal life; but the similitude is derived from hence, that the rock taketh away from us what hindereth us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Presume not therefore on thy strength: the firmness of the rock rubbeth off thy old age: for that Rock was Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Christ our youth shall be restored like that of the eagle.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord executeth mercy and judgment for all them that are oppressed with wrong” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6).…An adulterous woman is brought forward to be stoned according to the Law, but she is brought before the Lawgiver Himself.…Our Lord, at the time she was brought before Him, bending His Head, began writing on the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When He bent Himself down upon the earth, He then wrote on the earth: before He bent upon the earth, He wrote not on the earth, but on stone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The earth was now something fertile, ready to bring forth from the Lord’s letters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the stone He had written the Law, intimating the hardness of the Jews: He wrote on the earth, signifying the productiveness of Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then they who were leading the adulteress came, like raging waves against a rock: but they were dashed to pieces by His answer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He said to them, “He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And again bending His head, He began writing on the ground.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And now each man, when he asked his own conscience, came not forward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was not a weak adulterous woman, but their own adulterate conscience, that drove them back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They wished to punish, to judge; they came to the Rock, their judges were overthrown by the Rock.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Execute mercy to the wicked, not as being wicked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not receive the wicked, in so far forth as he is wicked: that is, do not receive him as if from inclination towards and love for his iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is forbidden to give unto a sinner, and to receive sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet how is this, “Give unto every man that asketh of thee”? and this, “if thine enemy hunger, feed him”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is seemingly contradictory: but it is opened to those who knock in the name of Christ, and will be clear unto those who seek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Help not a sinner:” and, “give not to the ungodly;” and yet, “give unto every man that asketh of thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is a sinner who asketh of me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give, not as unto a sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When dost thou give as unto a sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When that which maketh him a sinner, pleaseth thee so that thou givest.…Let those who give to a man who fights with wild beasts, tell me why they give?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why doth he give to this man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He loveth that in him, in which consists his greatest sin; this he feedeth, this he clotheth in him, wickedness itself, made public by all witnessing it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why doth the man give, who giveth to actors, or to charioteers, or to courtesans?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not these very persons give to human beings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is not the nature of God’s work that they attend to, but the iniquity of the human work.…When therefore thou givest, thou givest to infamy, not to bravery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As then he who giveth to the fighter of beasts, giveth not to the man, but to a most infamous profession; for if he were only a man, and not a fighter of beasts, thou wouldest not give; thou honourest him in vice, not nature: so on the other hand, if thou give to the righteous, if thou give to the prophet, if thou give to the disciple of Christ anything of which he is in want, without thinking that he is Christ’s disciple, that he is God’s minister, that he is God’s steward; but art thinking in that case of some temporal advantage, for instance, that when perchance he shall be needful to thy cause, he may be bought for thee, because thou hast given him something; thou hast no more given to the righteous, if thou hast thus given, than he gave to the man, when he gave to the beast-fighter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The matter, then, most beloved, is quite open to us, and I conceive, that although it was obscure, it is now clear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was to this that the Lord bound thee, when He said, “He who hath received the righteous man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That were enough.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as the righteous may be received with another intention,…He saith, “He who receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man:” that is, receiving him in consideration of his righteousness:…that is, because he is Christ’s disciple, because he is a steward of the Mystery: “Verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So understand, he who receiveth a sinner in the name of a sinner shall lose his reward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11. …On this account therefore be merciful without fear, extend love even unto thine enemies: punish those who chance to belong to thy government, restrain them with affection, with charity, in regard to their eternal salvation; lest while thou sparest the flesh, the soul perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do this: and though thou have to endure many, over whom thou canst not exercise discipline, because thou hast no lawful authority over them; bear their injuries; be without apprehension.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will show mercy unto thee if thou shalt have been merciful: thou shalt be merciful, without the injuries thou sufferest losing their punishment; “To Me belongeth vengeance, I will repay,” saith the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He made His ways known unto Moses” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7).…For the Law was given with this view, that the sick might be convinced of his infirmity, and pray for the physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the hidden way of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hadst long ago heard, “Who healeth all thine infirmities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their infirmities were as yet hidden in the sick; the five books were given to Moses: the pool was surrounded by five porches; he brought forth the sick, that they might lie there, that they might be made known, not that they might be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The five porches discovered, but healed not, the sick; the pool healed when one descended, and this when it was disturbed: the disturbance of the pool was in our Lord’s Passion.…Since therefore this is a mystery there, he teacheth that the Law was given that sinners might be convinced of their sin, and call upon the Physician in order to receive grace.…Therefore, as I had begun to say, because this is a great mystery in the Law, that it was given with this view, that by the increase of sin, the proud might be humbled, the humbled might confess, the confessing might be healed; these are the hidden ways, which He made known to Moses, through whom He gave the Law, by which sin should abound, that grace might more abound.…“He hath made known His good pleasure unto the children of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To all the children of Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the true children of Israel; yea, to all the children of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the treacherous, the insidious, the hypocrites, are not children of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who are the children of Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is full of compassion and mercy: long-suffering, and of great mercy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why so long-suffering?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why so great in mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men sin and live; sins are added on, life continueth: men blaspheme daily, and “He maketh His sun to rise over the good and the wicked.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On all sides He calleth to amendment, on all sides He calleth to repentance, He calleth by the blessings of creation, He calleth by giving time for life, He calleth through the reader, He calleth through the preacher, He calleth through the innermost thought by the rod of correction, He calleth by the mercy of consolation: “He is long-suffering, and of great mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But take heed lest by ill using the length of God’s mercy, thou treasure up for thyself, as the Apostle saith, wrath in the day of wrath.…For some there are who prepare to turn, and yet put it off, and in them crieth out the raven’s voice, “Cras!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The raven which was sent from the ark, never returned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God seeketh not procrastination in the raven’s voice, but confession in the wailing of the dove.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The dove, when sent forth, returned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How long, To-morrow!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look to thy last morrow: since thou knowest not what is thy last morrow, let it suffice that thou hast lived up to this day a sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, often thou art wont to hear, thou hast heard to-day also; daily thou hearest, and daily thou amendest not.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He will not alway be chiding: neither keepeth He His anger for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since it is in consequence of His anger that we live in the scourges and corruption of mortality: we have this in punishment for the first sin.…Is it not through His anger, my brethren, that “in the sweat of thy face and in toil thou shalt eat bread, and the earth shall bear thorns and thistles unto thee”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was said to our forefathers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or if our life is different from this; if thou canst, turn unto some pleasure, where thou mayest not feel thorns.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Choose what thou hast wished, whether thou art covetous or luxurious; to name these two alone; add a third passion, that of ambition; how great thorns are there in the desire of honours? in the luxury of lusts how great thorns? in the ardour of covetousness how great thorns?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What troubles are there in base loves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What terrible anxieties here in this life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I omit hell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beware lest thou even now become a hell unto thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole of this, my brethren, is the result of His anger: and when thou hast turned thyself unto works of righteousness, thou canst not but toil upon earth; and toil endeth not before life endeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We must toil on the way, that we may rejoice in our country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore consoleth by His promises thy toil, thy labours, thy troubles, saying to thee, “He will not alway be chiding.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath not dealt with us according to our sins” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks unto God, because He hath vouchsafed this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have not received what we were deserving of: “He hath not dealt with us according to our sins, nor rewarded us according to our wickednesses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For as the height of heaven above the earth, so hath the Lord confirmed His mercy toward them that fear Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe the heaven: everywhere on every side it covereth the earth, nor is there any part of the earth not covered by the heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men sin beneath heaven: they do all evil deeds beneath the heaven; yet they are covered by the heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is light for the eyes, thence air, thence breath, thence rain upon the earth for the sake of its fruits, thence all mercy from heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take away the aid of heaven from the earth: it will fail at once.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As then the protection of heaven abideth upon the earth, so doth the Lord’s protection abide upon them that fear Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou fearest God, His protection is above thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps thou art scourged, and conceivest that God hath forsaken thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath forsaken thee, if the protection of heaven hath forsaken the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look, how wide the east is from the west; so far hath He set our sins from us” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who know the Sacraments know this; nevertheless, I only say what all may hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When sin is remitted, thy sins fall, thy grace riseth; thy sins are as it were on the decline, thy grace which freeth thee on the rise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Truth springeth from the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy grace is born, thy sins fall, thou art in a certain manner made new.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shouldest look to the rising, and turn away from the setting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Turn away from thy sins, turn unto the grace of God; when thy sins fall, thou riseth and profitest.…One region of the heaven falleth, another riseth: but the region which is now rising will set after twelve hours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not like this is the grace which riseth unto us: both our sins fall for ever, and grace abideth for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea, like as a father pitieth his own children, even so hath the Lord had mercy on them that fear Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him be as angry as He shall will, He is our Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He hath scourged us, and afflicted us, and bruised us: He is our Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Son, if thou bewailest, wail beneath thy Father; do not so with indignation, do not so with the puffing up of pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What thou sufferest, whence thou mournest, it is medicine, not punishment; it is thy chastening, not thy condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not refuse the scourge, if thou dost not wish to be refused thy heritage: do not think of what punishment thou sufferest in the scourge, but what place thou hast in the Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He knoweth our forming” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 14): that is, our infirmity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knoweth what He hath created, how it hath fallen, how it may be repaired, how it may be adopted, how it may be enriched.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, we are made of clay: “The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He sent even His own Son, Him who was made the second man, Him who was God before all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He was second in His coming, first in His returning: He died after many, He arose before all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He knoweth our forming.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What forming?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why sayest thou that He knoweth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He hath pitied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember that we are but dust.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Addressing God Himself, he saith, “Remember,” as if God could forget: He perceiveth, He knoweth in such a manner that He cannot forget.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “Remember”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let thy mercy continue towards us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou knowest our forming; forget not our forming, lest we forget thy grace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Man, his days are but as grass” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let man consider what he is; let not man be proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His days are but as grass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is the grass proud, that is now flourishing, and in a very short space dried up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is the grass proud that flourisheth only for a brief season, until the sun be hot?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is then good for us that His mercy be upon us, and from grass make gold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he flourisheth as a flower of the field.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole splendour of the human race; honour, powers, riches, pride, threats, is the flower of the grass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That house flourisheth, and that family is great, that family flourisheth; and how many flourish, and how many years do they live!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many years to thee, are but a short season unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God doth not count, as thou dost.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Compared with the length and long life of ages, all the flower of any house is as the flower of the field.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the beauty of the year hardly lasteth for the year.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever there flourisheth, whatever there is warmed with heat, whatever there is beautiful, lasteth not; nay, it cannot exist for one whole year.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In how brief a season do flowers pass away, and these are the beauty of the herbs!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This which is so very beautiful, this quickly falleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Inasmuch then as He knoweth as a father our forming, that we are but grass, and can only flourish for a time; He sent unto us His Word, and His Word, which abideth for evermore, He hath made a brother unto the grass which abideth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wonder not that thou shalt be a sharer of His Eternity; He became Himself first a sharer of thy grass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will He who assumed from thee what was lowly, deny unto thee what is exalted in respect of thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The wind shall go over on it, it shall not be; and the place thereof shall know it no more” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he is not speaking of grass, but of that for whose sake even the Word became grass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou art man, and on thy account the Word became man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All flesh is grass:” “and the Word was made flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great then is the hope of the grass, since the Word hath been made flesh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which abideth for evermore, hath not disdained to assume grass, that the grass might not despair of itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In thy reflections therefore on thyself, think of thy low estate, think of thy dust: be not lifted up: if thou art anything better, thou wilt be so by His Grace, thou wilt be so by His mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hear what followeth: “but the mercy of the Lord endureth for ever and ever upon them that fear Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye who fear not Him, will be grass, and in grass, and in torment with the grass: for the flesh shall arise unto the torment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let those who fear Him rejoice, because His mercy is upon them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His righteousness upon children’s children” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He speaketh of reward, “upon children’s children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many servants of God are there who have not children, how much less children’s children?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He calleth our works our children; the reward of works, our “children’s children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even upon such as keep His covenant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let men beware that all may not conceive what is here said to belong to themselves: let them choose, while they have the choice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And keep in memory His commandments to do them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wast already disposed to flatter thyself, and perhaps to recite to me the Psalter, which I have not by heart, or from memory to say over the whole Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Clearly thou art better in point of memory than I, better than any righteous man who doth not know the Law word for word: but see that thou keep the commandments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how shouldest thou keep them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not by memory, but by life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Such as keep in memory His commandments:” not, to recite them; but, “to do them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And now perhaps each man’s soul is disturbed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who remembereth all the commandments of God? who remembereth all the writings of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, I wish not only to hold them in my memory, but also to do them in my works: but who remembereth them all?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not: He burdeneth thee not: “on two commandments hang all the Law and the Prophets.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath prepared His throne in heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who but Christ hath prepared His throne in heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who descended and ascended, He who died, and rose from the dead, He who lifted up to heaven the manhood He had assumed, hath Himself prepared His throne in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The throne is the seat of the Judge: observe therefore ye who hear, that “He hath prepared His throne in heaven.”…The kingdom is the Lord’s, and He shall be the Governor among the people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His kingdom shall rule over all.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bless ye the Lord, ye Angels of His, ye that are mighty in strength: ye that fulfil His word” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the word of God, then, thou art not righteous, nor faithful, unless when thou dost it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ye that are mighty in strength, ye that fulfil His commandment, and hearken unto the voice of His words.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bless ye the Lord, all ye His hosts: ye servants of His that do His pleasure” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All ye angels, all ye that are mighty in strength: ye that do His word: all ye His hosts, ye servants of His that do His pleasure, do ye, ye bless the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all they who live wickedly, though their tongues be silent, by their lips do curse the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth it profit if thy tongue singeth a hymn, while thy life breatheth sacrilege?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By living ill thou hast set many tongues to blasphemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy tongue is given to the hymn, the tongues of those who behold thee, to blasphemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then thou dost wish to bless the Lord, do His word, do His will.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bless ye the Lord, all ye works of His, in all places of His dominion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in every place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him not be blessed where He ruleth not: “in all places of His dominion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man perchance say: I cannot praise the Lord in the East, because He hath departed unto the West; or, I cannot praise Him in the West, because He is in the East.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor yet from the desert hills.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is the Judge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is everywhere, in such wise that everywhere He may be praised: He is in such wise on every side, that we may be joyful in Him on every side: He is in such wise blessed on every side, that on every side we may live well.…“In every place of His dominion: bless thou the Lord, O my soul!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The last verse is the same as the first: blessing is at the head of the Psalm, blessing at the end; from blessing we set out, to blessing let us return, in blessing let us reign.
